sections.content: To send a DSC distress alert, lift the protective cover over the distress button and press and hold it for approximately 5 seconds. The radio will transmit a distress alert on Channel 70 containing your MMSI, position (if GPS-connected), time, and nature of distress. After transmission, the radio automatically switches to Channel 16 for voice follow-up.

sections.title: Auto-Repeat
sections.content: Hold the distress button for 5 seconds. The radio will automatically repeat the distress alert every 3.5–4.5 minutes until it is acknowledged by a coast station.

sections.prompt: What should you do immediately after sending a DSC distress alert?

options.text: Turn off the radio to conserve battery
Begin a voice MAYDAY call on Channel 16
Send another DSC alert on a different channel

sections.explanation: After sending a DSC distress alert, the radio switches to Channel 16 where you should immediately begin a voice MAYDAY call with full details of your distress situation.
